reason: the counter is not the same time domain as scheduler-delivered current-thread CPU time

primary_source.title: Intel 64 and IA-32 Architectures Software Developer's Manual, Volume 3B

semantic_mismatch: REF_TSC counts only while the logical processor is outside halt and TM stop-clock states
it stops during thermal-throttling duty-off periods
Intel describes it as able to approximate elapsed time rather than as scheduler-delivered runtime
Linux task-clock and CLOCK_THREAD_CPUTIME_ID continue to represent CPU time delivered to the scheduled thread
conclusion: Do not benchmark or select reference cycles as an eligible ThreadCpuInstant candidate. A cheaper read cannot compensate for measuring a different quantity.
